title: hieracium pumilare
intro: Hieracium pumilare is a small perennial herb, also known as Mouse Ear Hawkweed or Dwarf Hawkweed, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Hieracium pumilare is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and allergic reactions.
